Question: What is the legal name of your organization?
Vendor Response: Wazuh Inc.

Question: Where is your company headquartered?
Vendor Response: San Francisco, CA, United States

Question: How many employees does your organization have?
Vendor Response: 87 full-time employees

Question: How long has your organization been in business?
Vendor Response: Founded 2022

Question: Describe the service you are providing to CloudVault Financial.
Vendor Response: Managed Wazuh SIEM-as-a-Service. We deploy, manage, and maintain Wazuh infrastructure for security monitoring, threat detection, and compliance reporting.

Question: What is the deployment model for your service?
Vendor Response: Multi-tenant SaaS hosted on AWS (us-east-1 and eu-west-1)

Question: Do you have a dedicated security team?
Vendor Response: Yes. Our security team consists of 4 full-time security engineers and a CISO.

Question: Who is your CISO or equivalent security leader?
Vendor Response: Marcus Chen, CISO. 15 years experience in cloud security.

Question: Do you have a SOC 2 Type II report?
Vendor Response: We achieved SOC 2 Type II certification in 2025. Our report was audited by A-LIGN and covers security
column6: and availability. Report available under NDA.

Question: Can you provide a copy of your most recent SOC 2 report?
Vendor Response: SOC 2 Type I report is available under NDA. Contact security@wazuh.com.

Question: Do you hold ISO 27001 certification?
Vendor Response: Not at this time. We are evaluating ISO 27001 for 2027.

Question: Are you FedRAMP authorized?
Vendor Response: No. FedRAMP authorization is not on our current roadmap.

Question: Do you comply with GDPR requirements?
Vendor Response: Yes. We maintain a Data Processing Agreement and comply with GDPR requirements for EU customers.

Question: Can you provide a HIPAA Business Associate Agreement?
Vendor Response: Not at this time. HIPAA compliance is on our 2027 roadmap. We do not currently accept PHI, and our platform should not be used to process PHI.

Question: Have you completed a third-party penetration test in the last 12 months?
Vendor Response: Yes. Our most recent third-party penetration test was completed in January 2026 by NCC Group. Summary report available under NDA.

Question: How often do you conduct penetration tests?
Vendor Response: Annually by a third party. We also conduct quarterly internal security assessments.

Question: Do you have cyber insurance?
Vendor Response: Yes. Cyber liability insurance with coverage up to $5M through Coalition.

Question: Do you maintain a risk register?
Vendor Response: Yes. Our risk register is reviewed quarterly by the security team and annually by the executive team.

Question: How is customer data encrypted at rest?
Vendor Response: All customer data is encrypted at rest using AES-256 via AWS S3 server-side encryption (SSE-S3). Database volumes use AWS EBS encryption with AWS-managed keys.

Question: How is customer data encrypted in transit?
Vendor Response: All data in transit is encrypted using TLS 1.2 or higher. We enforce TLS 1.2 minimum on all API endpoints and agent communications.

Question: Do you use customer-managed encryption keys?
Vendor Response: Not currently. We use AWS-managed encryption keys. Customer-managed KMS keys are on our Enterprise plan roadmap.

Question: Is customer data logically or physically segregated from other customers?
Vendor Response: Customer data is logically segregated using unique tenant identifiers, separate database schemas, and tenant-scoped access controls. We do not offer physical segregation.

Question: Where is customer data stored geographically?
Vendor Response: US customers: AWS us-east-1 (Virginia). EU customers can request eu-west-1 (Ireland). We do not replicate data across regions without customer consent.

Question: Do you process or store data outside the United States?
Vendor Response: Only for EU customers who explicitly request eu-west-1. US customer data remains in the US.

Question: What is your data retention policy?
Vendor Response: Customer log data is retained for 90 days by default. Customers can configure retention up to 365 days at additional cost. Retention policies are configurable per data type.

Question: What happens to customer data upon contract termination?
Vendor Response: Customer data is deleted within 30 days of contract termination. Customers can request a full data export in JSON format before termination. Deletion is confirmed in writing.

Question: Can customers request deletion of specific data?
Vendor Response: Yes. Customers can submit data deletion requests through our API or support portal. Deletion is completed within 14 business days.

Question: How do you ensure data is completely removed when deleted?
Vendor Response: We perform logical deletion immediately and physical deletion within 30 days. AWS S3 object versioning is disabled for customer data buckets. We do not maintain backups beyond 30 days.

Question: Do you perform data classification?
Vendor Response: Yes. We classify data into four categories: Public, Internal, Confidential, and Restricted. Customer security log data is classified as Confidential.

Question: How is customer data backed up?
Vendor Response: Customer data is backed up daily to a separate AWS availability zone. Backups are encrypted using the same encryption standards as production data. Backup retention is 30 days.

Question: How do you manage user authentication for customer access?
Vendor Response: We support SSO via SAML 2.0 and OIDC. Username/password authentication is also available with configurable password complexity requirements.

Question: Is multi-factor authentication supported?
Vendor Response: MFA is available for all customer user accounts via TOTP authenticator apps. MFA is not enforced by default but can be mandated by customer administrators.

Question: Do you enforce MFA for your internal staff?
Vendor Response: Yes. All internal staff are required to use hardware security keys (YubiKey) for authentication to production systems and corporate SSO.

Question: How do you manage privileged access internally?
Vendor Response: We follow the principle of least privilege. Administrative access to production systems requires MFA, is time-limited (4-hour sessions), and is reviewed quarterly.

Question: How do you manage service accounts and API keys?
Vendor Response: API keys are generated per customer and can be rotated by the customer at any time. Service accounts use short-lived tokens with automatic rotation. No long-lived credentials are used in production.

Question: Do you perform regular access reviews?
Vendor Response: We conduct quarterly access reviews for all internal staff with production access. Results are documented and available upon request.

Question: How many employees have access to customer data?
Vendor Response: 8 employees have the ability to access customer data in production: 4 security engineers, 2 SRE engineers, 1 support lead, and the CISO. All access is logged and reviewed.

Question: How do you handle employee offboarding?
Vendor Response: Access is revoked within 4 hours of termination notification. Offboarding checklist includes credential revocation across all systems. We maintain offboarding logs.

Question: Can customer data be accessed by vendor employees?
Vendor Response: Access to customer data is restricted to authorized personnel on a need-to-know basis. All access to customer data is logged in our internal audit trail. Customers can request access logs.

Question: Do you support IP allowlisting for API access?
Vendor Response: Yes. Customers can configure IP allowlists for API access through the administration console.

Question: What cloud provider do you use?
Vendor Response: AWS. We leverage multiple availability zones within each region for redundancy.

Question: How do you manage vulnerabilities in your infrastructure?
Vendor Response: We run automated vulnerability scans weekly using Qualys. Critical vulnerabilities (CVSS 9.0+) are patched within 48 hours. High vulnerabilities within 7 days. Medium within 30 days.

Question: Do you use a WAF or DDoS protection?
Vendor Response: Yes. AWS WAF and AWS Shield Standard are deployed on all customer-facing endpoints. We maintain custom WAF rules for OWASP Top 10 protections.

Question: How do you secure your container infrastructure?
Vendor Response: We use Amazon ECS with Fargate for container workloads. Container images are scanned for vulnerabilities before deployment using ECR image scanning. We follow CIS Docker Benchmark guidelines.

Question: Do you have a network segmentation strategy?
Vendor Response: Yes. Our environment is segmented using VPCs, security groups, and network ACLs. Customer-facing services, internal services, and management networks are on separate VPC segments.

Question: How do you monitor your own infrastructure?
Vendor Response: We use our own Wazuh deployment for security monitoring. Additionally we use AWS GuardDuty for threat detection and CloudTrail for API audit logging.

Question: How do you manage secrets and credentials?
Vendor Response: All secrets are stored in AWS Secrets Manager with automatic rotation enabled. No secrets are stored in code repositories, configuration files, or environment variables.

Question: Do you have an intrusion detection/prevention system?
Vendor Response: Yes. Wazuh provides host-based intrusion detection on all production instances. AWS GuardDuty provides network-level threat detection.

Question: How do you handle logging for your own infrastructure?
Vendor Response: All infrastructure logs are centralized in our internal Wazuh deployment. CloudTrail logs are stored in a dedicated, immutable S3 bucket with a 365-day retention policy.

Question: Do you perform baseline hardening on your systems?
Vendor Response: Yes. All production systems are hardened according to CIS benchmarks. We use automated configuration management to enforce baseline compliance.

Question: Do you have a documented incident response plan?
Vendor Response: Yes. Our IR plan is based on NIST SP 800-61 and is reviewed and updated annually. The plan was last tested via tabletop exercise in March 2026.

Question: How quickly do you notify customers of a security incident?
Vendor Response: We notify affected customers within 72 hours of confirming a security incident that impacts customer data. For critical incidents, we provide initial notification within 24 hours.

Question: Have you experienced a data breach in the last 3 years?
Vendor Response: We have not experienced a data breach affecting customer data since the company was founded in 2022.

Question: Do you have a dedicated incident response team?
Vendor Response: Yes. Our IR team consists of the CISO, 2 senior security engineers, and 1 SRE engineer. On-call rotation provides 24/7 coverage.

Question: How do you communicate with customers during an incident?
Vendor Response: We provide updates via email to designated security contacts and through our status page. For critical incidents we offer direct phone communication with the IR team lead.

Question: Do you conduct post-incident reviews?
Vendor Response: Yes. We conduct a post-incident review within 5 business days of incident closure. Lessons learned are documented and shared with affected customers upon request.

Question: What is your guaranteed uptime SLA?
Vendor Response: 99.9% uptime, measured monthly. SLA credits are issued for availability below the guaranteed threshold.

Question: What is your RTO and RPO?
Vendor Response: RTO is 4 hours. RPO is 1 hour for all customer data.

Question: Do you have a disaster recovery plan?
Vendor Response: Yes. We maintain a DR plan with failover to a secondary AWS region (us-west-2 for US customers). DR tests are conducted annually.

Question: How often do you test your DR plan?
Vendor Response: Annually. Our last DR test was completed in February 2026. Test results are available upon request.

Question: Do you have a business continuity plan?
Vendor Response: Yes. Our BCP covers loss of key personnel and extended service outages. It is reviewed annually.

Question: Do you use subprocessors?
Vendor Response: Yes. We use AWS for infrastructure hosting and Datadog for internal infrastructure monitoring and PagerDuty for incident alerting.

Question: How do you assess the security of your subprocessors?
Vendor Response: We review subprocessor SOC 2 reports annually. We maintain data processing agreements with all subprocessors. We conduct annual security reviews.

Question: Do you notify customers of changes to subprocessors?
Vendor Response: Yes. We provide 30 days advance notice of any new subprocessor that will have access to customer data.

Question: Do any subprocessors have access to customer data?
Vendor Response: AWS has access as the infrastructure provider. Datadog and PagerDuty do not have access to customer security log data. Our monitoring tools only collect infrastructure metrics, not customer data.

Question: Do you perform regular code reviews?
Vendor Response: All code changes go through mandatory peer review and automated SAST scanning (Semgrep) before deployment. No code reaches production without review.

Question: Do you have a secure software development lifecycle?
Vendor Response: We follow an SDLC based on OWASP SAMM. This includes threat modeling for new features and security-focused design reviews.

Question: How often do you release updates?
Vendor Response: We deploy updates bi-weekly using a blue-green deployment strategy. Emergency security patches can be deployed within 4 hours.

Question: Do you have a bug bounty or vulnerability disclosure program?
Vendor Response: We have a responsible disclosure policy published on our website. We do not currently operate a formal bug bounty program.

Question: How do you manage dependencies and third-party libraries?
Vendor Response: We use Dependabot for automated dependency updates and Snyk for vulnerability scanning of third-party libraries. Critical dependency vulnerabilities are patched within 48 hours.

Question: Do you perform dynamic application security testing?
Vendor Response: Yes. We conduct quarterly DAST scans using OWASP ZAP against our staging environment. Findings are triaged and remediated before production deployment.

Question: Do you perform background checks on employees?
Vendor Response: Yes. All employees undergo criminal background checks and employment verification before hiring.

Question: Do you provide security awareness training?
Vendor Response: All employees complete security awareness training during onboarding and annually thereafter. We conduct quarterly phishing simulations with a target click rate below 5%.

Question: Do employees sign confidentiality agreements?
Vendor Response: Yes. All employees sign NDAs and confidentiality agreements as part of their employment terms.

Question: How do you handle security violations by employees?
Vendor Response: Security violations are handled according to our disciplinary policy. Serious violations can result in immediate termination and are reported to law enforcement if criminal activity is suspected.

Question: Do you monitor your own infrastructure for security events?
Vendor Response: Yes. We use our own Wazuh deployment for security monitoring of all production infrastructure. AWS GuardDuty and CloudTrail provide additional detection capabilities.

Question: How long do you retain security logs?
Vendor Response: Internal security logs are retained for 365 days. CloudTrail logs are retained indefinitely in S3 Glacier.

Question: Can customers access audit logs for their own tenant?
Vendor Response: Yes. Customers can access their tenant audit logs via the API or dashboard. Logs include all user actions and system events within their tenant.

Question: Do you have a SIEM deployed?
Vendor Response: Yes. We operate our own Wazuh deployment for internal security monitoring, separate from customer environments.

Question: How do you detect anomalous behavior?
Vendor Response: We use Wazuh rules for signature-based detection and AWS GuardDuty for anomaly-based detection. Custom detection rules are maintained for known attack patterns against our platform.

Question: Do you have a privacy policy?
Vendor Response: Yes. Our privacy policy is publicly available on our website at wazuh.com/privacy.

Question: Do you have a Data Protection Officer?
Vendor Response: Our CISO serves as our Data Protection Officer for GDPR compliance purposes.

Question: Do you process data in accordance with customer instructions only?
Vendor Response: Yes. We process customer data only as instructed by the customer and as defined in our Data Processing Agreement. We do not use customer data for our own purposes, training, or analytics.

Question: Do you transfer data internationally?
Vendor Response: Only for EU customers who request the eu-west-1 region. We do not transfer US customer data outside the US. We maintain Standard Contractual Clauses for EU data transfers.

Question: Can customers request a copy of all their data?
Vendor Response: Yes. Customers can request a full data export in JSON format at any time through the API or by contacting support.

Question: How do you manage changes to your production environment?
Vendor Response: All changes follow our change management process including peer review, automated testing, staging validation, and approval by a senior engineer. Changes are logged in our change management system.

Question: Do you maintain a change log?
Vendor Response: Yes. All production changes are logged with timestamps and the identity of the person who made the change. Change logs are retained for 365 days.

Question: How do you handle emergency changes?
Vendor Response: Emergency changes follow an expedited process requiring approval from the on-call SRE lead and the CISO. Emergency changes are reviewed retrospectively within 24 hours.

Question: Do you notify customers of changes that may affect them?
Vendor Response: Yes. Planned maintenance is communicated 7 days in advance via email and our status page. Changes that affect functionality are documented in release notes.

Question: How do you secure your API endpoints?
Vendor Response: APIs use OAuth 2.0 authentication with short-lived access tokens. Rate limiting is enforced per customer. All inputs are validated against defined schemas.

Question: Do you have API documentation?
Vendor Response: Yes. Complete API documentation is available at docs.wazuh.com/api.

Question: How do you handle API versioning?
Vendor Response: We maintain backward compatibility for at least 12 months after deprecation notice. Breaking changes are communicated 90 days in advance.

Question: Do you support webhook integrations?
Vendor Response: Yes. Customers can configure webhooks for alerting and integration with their existing tools. Webhook payloads are signed with HMAC-SHA256 for verification.

Question: What is your average support response time?
Vendor Response: Standard support: 8 business hours. Priority support: 2 hours. Critical incidents: 30 minutes.

Question: Do you offer 24/7 support?
Vendor Response: 24/7 support is available for Critical and High severity incidents. Standard support hours are Monday-Friday, 8am-8pm ET.

Question: Do you offer a dedicated customer success manager?
Vendor Response: For Enterprise plans ($10K+/year), yes. Standard plan support is ticket-based.

Question: What is the minimum contract term?
Vendor Response: Annual contracts. Month-to-month available at a premium.

Question: Do you carry errors and omissions insurance?
Vendor Response: Yes. E&O insurance with $3M coverage.

Question: Can we conduct our own security assessment of your service?
Vendor Response: We allow customer security assessments under our responsible testing policy. Please contact security@wazuh.com to coordinate testing scope and schedule.

Question: What are your limitation of liability terms?
Vendor Response: Limitation of liability is capped at the total fees paid by the customer in the 12 months preceding the claim. We do not limit liability for breaches of confidentiality obligations or data protection failures.
